page: Amazon Web Services r8gb.16xlarge is a memory-optimized server powered by custom AWS Graviton processors. Built on the ARMv9 architecture using Neoverse-V2 cores, this instance features 64 dedicated physical cores (64 vCPUs) running at 2.8 GHz with no hyperthreading. It is equipped with 512.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The server operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 133 Gbps. There is no local storage or GPU hardware bundled with this instance.

Performance benchmarks highlight the server's exceptional memory subsystem. It achieves top-tier results in memory bandwidth tests for both small and large block read/write operations, as well as in PassMark memory scores. This high memory throughput translates directly into top-tier performance for database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput. Multi-core compute performance is strong, as evidenced by top-tier Geekbench multi-core and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression results, though single-core performance remains in the average tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture delivers high resource density and memory bandwidth relative to its hardware profile, making it highly efficient for memory-bound workloads. However, the lack of local NVMe storage requires relying on network-attached storage, which is a key architectural tradeoff. The server is highly suited for memory-intensive applications, including in-memory databases, large-scale caching layers, data analytics, and multi-threaded enterprise applications.
description: Amazon Web Services r8gb.16xlarge is a memory-optimized ARM64 server powered by 64 dedicated AWS Graviton (Neoverse-V2) physical cores running at 2.8 GHz. It features 512.0 GB of system memory, providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio, and delivers 133 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmarks show top-tier memory bandwidth, Redis SET operations, and database performance, alongside strong multi-core compute capabilities. The server offers high resource density and memory throughput, making it an efficient choice for memory-bound workloads. It is highly suited for in-memory databases, large-scale caching, and data analytics.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8gb.16xlarge is a memory-optimized ARM64 server featuring 64 dedicated Graviton cores, 512 GB RAM, and 133 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier memory and database performance.

tagline: An ARM64 memory-optimized server featuring sixty-four dedicated cores and top-tier memory bandwidth for demanding database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated ARM64 AWS Graviton physical cores running at 2.8 GHz
512.0 GB of system memory delivering top-tier read and write bandwidth
133 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier performance in Redis SET and database operations
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and data analytics
